description: RAG knowledge base for Personal Finance Advisor (Step 11)

documents.title: 50-20-30 Budgeting Rule
documents.content: The 50-20-30 rule allocates 50% of net income to needs (rent, food, utilities, EMI), 20% to savings and investments, and 30% to wants (shopping, entertainment, travel). It provides a simple framework for balanced budgeting without complex spreadsheets.

documents.title: Emergency Fund Basics
documents.content: An emergency fund should cover 3-6 months of essential expenses. Park it in a high-yield savings account or liquid mutual fund. Treat it as a non-negotiable monthly transfer, not optional. Aim to build it before investing in equity.

documents.title: Reducing Food Expenses
documents.content: Meal prepping on weekends can reduce weekly food costs by 25-35%. Using a grocery list and avoiding impulse buys at the store saves an average of 20% per trip. Cooking at home is 5x cheaper than ordering in on average.

documents.title: EMI Management Strategy
documents.content: EMIs should not exceed 40-50% of net monthly income. Prioritise high-interest loans (personal loans, credit cards) for prepayment. Use windfalls (bonus, tax refund) to reduce principal. Avoid taking new loans while existing EMIs are active.

documents.title: Investment Ladder for Beginners
documents.content: Start with: (1) Emergency fund in liquid savings, (2) PPF or NPS for tax benefits, (3) Index mutual funds via SIP for long-term wealth. A simple 3-fund portfolio (large cap index, mid cap index, short bond fund) is sufficient for most retail investors.

documents.title: Credit Card Best Practices
documents.content: Always pay the full statement balance, never just the minimum. This avoids 36-45% annual interest on the revolving balance. Use credit cards only for pre-planned purchases. Set up auto-pay for the full amount to avoid late fees.

documents.title: Subscription Audit
documents.content: The average Indian household pays for 3-4 streaming services but actively uses only 1-2. Review all recurring charges on your bank statement quarterly. Cancel unused gym memberships — most gyms charge cancellation fees only within the first 30 days.

documents.title: Travel Hacking for Savings
documents.content: Book domestic flights 6-8 weeks in advance. Use airline miles credit cards to earn points on all purchases. Consider train travel for distances under 500 km — it is 60-80% cheaper. Compare hotel vs Airbnb pricing 2 weeks before a trip for best rates.
